The '''60th''' annual '''[[Berlin International Film Festival]]''' was held from 11 to 21 February 2010, with [[Werner Herzog]] as president of the jury.<ref name="Berlinale President">{{cite web|url=http://www.berlinale.de/en/presse/pressemitteilungen/alle/Alle-Detail_5364.html |title=Werner Herzog to be President of the Jury of the 60th Berlinale |access-date=22 December 2009 |work=berlinale.de |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091125161234/http://www.berlinale.de/en/presse/pressemitteilungen/alle/Alle-Detail_5364.html |archive-date=25 November 2009 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="thelocal.de">{{cite web |url=http://www.thelocal.de/society/20091119-23385.html |title=Werner Herzog to head Berlin film festival jury |access-date=22 December 2009 |work=thelocal.de| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20091120175701/http://www.thelocal.de/society/20091119-23385.html| archive-date= 20 November 2009 | url-status= live}}</ref><ref name="bbc news">{{cite news |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/entertainment/8370440.stm |title=Werner Herzog is to head the Berlin Film Festival jury |access-date=22 December 2009 |work=bbc news | date=20 November 2009| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20091123114011/http://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/entertainment/8370440.stm| archive-date= 23 November 2009 | url-status= live}}</ref> The opening film of the festival was Chinese director [[Wang Quan'an]]'s romantic drama ''[[Apart Together]]'', in competition, while the closing film is Japanese director [[Yoji Yamada]]'s ''[[Otōto (2010 film)|About Her Brother]]'', which was screened out of competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.indiewire.com/article/berlin_comes_together_again_for_chinese_opener |title=With Chinese Opener, Berlin Comes "Together" for 60th Fest |date=11 February 2010 |access-date=10 June 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.indiewire.com/photo/berlin |title=Berlin Fest Reveals Full Competition Slate for 60th Anniversary |date=17 July 2009 |access-date=10 June 2014 |archive-date=4 March 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304053005/http://www.indiewire.com/photo/berlin |url-status=dead }}</ref>

The [[Golden Bear]] went to Turkish film ''[[Honey (2010 film)|Bal]]'' directed by [[Semih Kaplanoğlu]]. A new record attendance was established with 282,000 sold tickets, according to the organizers. A restored version of [[Fritz Lang]]'s ''[[Metropolis (1927 film)|Metropolis]]'' was also shown at the festival.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bloomberg.com/apps/news?pid=20601088&sid=aLZf0c26Zuvc|title=Polanski's Ghost, Scorsese's Island to Debut at Berlin Festival|last=Hickley|first=Catherine|date=7 February 2010|publisher=Bloomberg.com|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110604061011/http://www.bloomberg.com/apps/news?pid=newsarchive&sid=aLZf0c26Zuvc|archive-date=4 June 2011|url-status=dead|access-date=1 November 2018|quote=A restored version of Fritz Lang’s original 1927 “Metropolis,” including negatives that were believed lost until they came to light in Buenos Aires in 2008, will be screened at the Brandenburg Gate on Feb. 12.}}</ref>

==Juries==
[[File:Werner Herzog 2009.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Werner Herzog]], Jury President]]
The following people were announced as being on the jury for the festival:<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2010/04_jury_2010/04_Jury_2010.html |title=JURIES 2010 |access-date=10 June 2014 |work=berlinale.de | archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20131021203858/http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2010/04_jury_2010/04_Jury_2010.html| archive-date=21 October 2013 | url-status= live}}</ref>

=== Main Competition ===
* [[Werner Herzog]], German filmmaker - Jury President
* [[Francesca Comencini]], Italy director and screenwriter
* [[Nuruddin Farah]], Somali writer
* [[Cornelia Froboess]], German actress and singer
* [[José María Morales (film producer)|José María Morales]], Spanish producer
* [[Yu Nan]], Chinese actress
* [[Renée Zellweger]], American actress

=== Best First Feature Award ===
* [[Michael Verhoeven]], German actor, director and screenwriter
* [[Ben Foster (actor)|Ben Foster]], American actor
* Lorna Tee, Malay producer

=== Short Film Competition ===
* Zita Carvalhosa, Brazilian producer
* Max Dax, German publisher and producer
* Samm Haillay, British producer

==Official Awards==

=== Main Competition ===
The following prizes were awarded by the Jury:<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2010/03_preistr_ger_2010/03_Preistraeger_2010.html |title=PRIZES & HONOURS 2010|access-date=10 June 2014 |work=berlinale.de| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131015120950/http://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2010/03_preistr_ger_2010/03_Preistraeger_2010.html| archive-date=15 October 2013 | url-status= live}}</ref>

* '''[[Golden Bear]]''': ''[[Honey (2010 film)|Bal]]'' by [[Semih Kaplanoğlu]]
* [[Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize]]: ''[[If I Want to Whistle, I Whistle]]'' by [[Florin Șerban]]
* [[Silver Bear for Best Director]]: [[Roman Polanski]] for ''[[The Ghost Writer (film)|The Ghost Writer]]''
* [[Silver Bear for Best Actress]]: [[Shinobu Terajima]] for ''[[Caterpillar (2010 film)|Caterpillar]]''
* [[Silver Bear for Best Actor]]: [[Grigoriy Dobrygin]] and [[Sergei Puskepalis]] for ''[[How I Ended This Summer]]''
* [[Silver Bear for Best Screenplay]]: [[Jin Na (screenwriter)|Jin Na]] and [[Wang Quan'an]] for ''[[Apart Together]]''
* '''Outstanding Artistic Contribution (Camera)''': Pavel Kostomarov for ''[[How I Ended This Summer]]''
* [[Alfred Bauer Prize]]: ''[[If I Want to Whistle, I Whistle]]'' by [[Florin Șerban]]

=== [[Golden Bear#Golden Bear – Honorary Award|Honorary Golden Bear]] ===
*[[Wolfgang Kohlhaase]]
*[[Hanna Schygulla]]

*Ulrich Gregor and Erika Gregor
*Fine Art Foundry Noack
